what: A source is a single URL
title: Remote sources: storage, authentication and windows
text: libraries like obstore / async-tiff separate store (bucket, region, credentials) path (object key), cptkirk takes one self-contained URL works rest. scheme (, AWS, host) selects backend; rest object key. cog_info() reads header IFDs — pixels — cheap way understand file fetching anything.

title: Remote sources: storage, authentication and windows
text: cptkirk never takes credentials arguments; reads environment, secrets stay scripts. honours variables GDAL’s /vsi* drivers, setup already works GDAL needs change: AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID / AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY / AWS_REGION, AWS_NO_SIGN_REQUEST=YES public buckets, AWS_S3_ENDPOINT custom endpoint, GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS, AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT, . object_store-native spellings (AWS_SKIP_SIGNATURE, …) work ; two differ, cptkirk translates GDAL name. non-secret knobs translated R-side – credentials pass straight environment reader. Mapping obstore store object: Two situations: Plain https:// non-AWS host (AEF tile , GEBCO ) uses HTTP backend needs nothing. AWS-hosted data — whether write s3://… https://….amazonaws.com/… — routes S3 backend, signs requests. public bucket, switch signing set region: selection host, scheme: *.amazonaws.com URL treated S3 even https, still needs -sign flag . Either spelling works — AWS_SKIP_SIGNATURE = "true" (object_store) AWS_NO_SIGN_REQUEST = "YES" (GDAL); cptkirk normalises GDAL YES/form .
code: Sys.setenv(AWS_REGION = "us-west-2", AWS_SKIP_SIGNATURE = "true")  scene <- paste0(   "s3://sentinel-cogs/sentinel-s2-l2a-cogs/12/S/UF/2022/6/",   "S2B_12SUF_20220609_0_L2A/" ) cog_info(paste0(scene, "TCI.tif")) #> ── cog_info ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> <s3://sentinel-cogs/sentinel-s2-l2a-cogs/12/S/UF/2022/6/S2B_12SUF_20220609_0_L2A/TCI.tif> #> size: 10980 x 10980 px (120.6 Mpx) #> bands: 3 Byte #> resolution: 10 (CRS units) #> crs: EPSG:32612 #> nodata: 0 #> overviews: 4 (10980x10980, 5490x5490, 2745x2745, 1373x1373, 687x687) #> band names: "band_01", "band_02", and "band_03"

title: Remote sources: storage, authentication and windows
text: ck_read() ck_warp() returns base-R array instead writing file. t_srs tr reads native window directly (GDAL); returned matrix/array carries geotransform, crs nodata attributes, self-describing. think source pixel offsets (like async-tiff’s Window(col_off, row_off, width, height)), convert target extent geotransform hand ck_read(): array maps onto async-tiff’s RasterArray: values data, dim() shape, attr(, "geotransform") transform, attr(, "crs") crs, attr(, "nodata") stands mask (derive one == attr(, "nodata")).
code: gt <- cog_info(paste0(scene, "B04.tif"))$geotransform  col_off <- 4096L; row_off <- 2048L; width <- 512L; height <- 512L te <- c(gt[1] +  col_off            * gt[2],   # xmin         gt[4] + (row_off + height)  * gt[6],   # ymin (gt[6] is negative)         gt[1] + (col_off + width)   * gt[2],   # xmax         gt[4] +  row_off            * gt[6])   # ymax  a <- ck_read(paste0(scene, "B04.tif"), te = te)   # native window, no reprojection dim(a) #> [1] 512 512 str(attributes(a)) #> List of 4 #>  $ dim         : int [1:2] 512 512 #>  $ geotransform: num [1:6] 340960 10 0 4079560 0 ... #>  $ crs         : chr "EPSG:32612" #>  $ nodata      : num 0

what: Building a stack from per-band COGs
title: Remote sources: storage, authentication and windows
text: Sentinel-2 L2A stores one COG per band, band’s native resolution (10 m visible/NIR bands, 20 m SWIR bands). assemble analysis-ready stack read band onto grid. ck_read() aligns output tr grid default, bands fetched te/tr come back co-registered — even native resolutions differ, GDAL resamples onto common grid: (cptkirk’s multi-source form, ck_warp(src = c(tile_a, tile_b), …), spatial mosaic data across adjacent tiles — band stack, combining separate band files “read , bind” .)
code: te20 <- c(gt[1] + 4096 * gt[2], gt[4] - (4096 + 512) * abs(gt[6]),           gt[1] + (4096 + 512) * gt[2], gt[4] - 4096 * abs(gt[6]))  bands <- c(B08 = "B08.tif", B04 = "B04.tif", B11 = "B11.tif")  # 10 m, 10 m, 20 m layers <- lapply(bands, function(b) {   ck_read(paste0(scene, b), te = te20, tr = c(20, 20), r = "bilinear") }) vapply(layers, dim, integer(2))         # identical dims -> co-registered #>      B08 B04 B11 #> [1,] 256 256 256 #> [2,] 256 256 256  stack <- array(0, dim = c(dim(layers[[1]]), length(layers))) for (k in seq_along(layers)) stack[, , k] <- layers[[k]] dim(stack) #> [1] 256 256   3

title: Remote sources: storage, authentication and windows
text: call warps reads. GEBCO global EPSG:4326; pull region reprojected Web Mercator resampled 2 km, fetching overview window target needs:  remote read fetched tiles covering extent, overview matching 2 km, handed reprojection resampling GDAL.
code: gebco <- "https://projects.pawsey.org.au/idea-gebco-tif/GEBCO_2024.tif"  out <- ck_warp(   gebco, tempfile(fileext = ".tif"),   t_srs = "EPSG:3857",   te    = c(110, -45, 155, -10),   # Australia, in EPSG:4326 ...   te_srs = "EPSG:4326",            # ... so name the extent's CRS   tr    = c(2000, 2000),   r     = "average" ) #> ! GDAL WARNING 1: Clamping output bounds to (-20037508.342789,-20037508.342789) -> (20037508.342789, 20037508.342789) ds <- new(gdalraster::GDALRaster, out) gdalraster::plot_raster(ds, legend = TRUE) ds$close()

title: Authors and Citation
text: Hugh Graham. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation

code: @Manual{,   title = {cptkirk: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs},   author = {Hugh Graham},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/belian-earth/cptkirk}, }

what: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs
title: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs
text: “Warp speed!” cptkirk warps remote Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs fast: saturates network concurrent reads, hands warp GDAL. combines two tools best--class one thing: async-tiff saturates remote byte-range reads (Cloud-Optimised) GeoTIFFs decodes tiles concurrently. GDAL (via gdalraster) best warper . cptkirk thin pipe . gdalwarp-style request works exactly source pixels warp need, streams tiles async-tiff appropriate overview level, stages -memory GDAL source, hands actual reprojection resampling GDAL. reimplements none GDAL’s warp logic; sizes saturates fetch.

title: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs
text: cptkirk’s single lever /O concurrency: wins scattering many small byte-range reads keeping network saturated. pays multi-band rasters, bands = streams subset band planes concurrently. Embedding stacks tens bands case built . single-band asset band subset exploit read one contiguous tile stream per window: exactly case GDAL’s /vsicurl already handles well, cptkirk adds little . Reach cptkirk source multi-band; single-band sources plain GDAL usually just fast.

title: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs
text: ck_warp() recommended, batteries-included entry point, named t_srs / te / tr / ts / r / bands arguments plus sensible defaults (multi-threaded warp, generous working memory, tr-grid-aligned output tiles resolution stack cleanly). warp_remote() faithful, defaults-free sibling gdalraster::warp(), src / dst / t_srs / cl_arg call shape source streamed remotely. Reach want GDAL’s exact behaviour full control via cl_arg. validate request tiny -memory probe fetching, bad CRS, resampling method creation option fails milliseconds instead download.

title: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs
code: # requires a Rust toolchain (rustc >= 1.78) and GDAL (via gdalraster) pak::pak("belian-earth/cptkirk")

title: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs
text: t_srs / te / tr / ts / r follow gdalwarp interface, extra raw flags pass straight via cl_arg. ck_warp() aligns output tr grid default (gdalwarp -tap); set tap = FALSE, use warp_remote(), GDAL’s exact requested extent.
code: library(cptkirk)  # inspect a remote COG (header + IFDs only, no pixels fetched) url <- paste0(   "https://data.source.coop/tge-labs/aef/v1/annual/2021/36S/",   "xekh5rjs4wg6wb9b4-0000000000-0000000000.tiff" )  (m <- cog_info(url)) #> ── cog_info ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> <https://data.source.coop/tge-labs/aef/v1/annual/2021/36S/xekh5rjs4wg6wb9b4-0000000000-0000000000.tiff> #> size: 8192 x 8192 px (67.1 Mpx) #> bands: 64 Int8 #> resolution: 10 (CRS units) #> crs: EPSG:32736 #> nodata: -128 #> overviews: 13 (8192x8192, 4096x4096, 2048x2048, 1024x1024, 512x512, 256x256, #> 128x128, 64x64, 32x32, 16x16, 8x8, 4x4, 2x2, 1x1) #> band names: "A00", "A01", "A02", "A03", "A04", "A05", …, "A62", and "A63"  # warp just a ~6 km window of the tile -- only the source tiles covering this # extent are streamed gt <- m$geotransform x0 <- gt[1] + 4000 * gt[2] y0 <- gt[4] + 4000 * gt[6] te <- c(x0, y0 - 8000, x0 + 8000, y0)   # AOI in the tile's CRS (EPSG:32736)  r <- ck_warp(   src   = url,   dst   = tempfile(fileext = ".tif"),   te    = te,                # area of interest   tr    = c(15, 15),         # 15 m output (an overview is selected)   r     = "average",   bands = c(11,10,9)         # subset bands at the fetch )  ds <- new(gdalraster::GDALRaster, r) gdalraster::plot_raster(ds) ds$close()

title: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs
text: ck_read() ck_warp() hands back base-R array — carrying geotransform / crs / nodata attributes — instead writing file, handy quick extraction inspection: Warping many areas raster? Open cog_source() pass handle place URL pay metadata round-trips .
code: a <- ck_read(url, te = te, tr = c(30, 30), bands = c(11, 10, 9)) dim(a)                # c(nrow, ncol, nband) #> [1] 267 267   3 names(attributes(a))  # dim, plus geotransform / crs / nodata #> [1] "dim"          "geotransform" "crs"          "nodata"

title: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs
text: Remote object-store sources (s3://, gs://, az://) authenticate process environment – cptkirk reads variables GDAL’s /vsi* drivers, existing GDAL setup drop-: names shared object_store forward verbatim; handful differ (AWS_S3_ENDPOINT, AWS_NO_SIGN_REQUEST, Azure account name, …) translated . Settings read environment gdalraster::set_config_option(). credential ever passes R – non-secret knobs (endpoints, -sign flags) translated R-side; secrets go straight environment reader. static credentials usual chain (web-identity, ECS, EC2 instance metadata) still applies.

what: How it picks what to fetch
title: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs
text: Window. target extent reprojected source CRS (edge densification) find source-pixel window output covers. Overview. target resolution mapped source units pick finest overview level whose decimation exceed output needs. Bands. bands = subsets fetch, requested bands’ bytes streamed. streamed window becomes -memory GDAL dataset; GDAL rest.

what: Tidy one-row-per-source summary of cog_info metadata — as.data.frame.cog_info
title: Tidy one-row-per-source summary of cog_info metadata — as.data.frame.cog_info
text: Flattens cog_info() output data frame columns src, width, height, n_bands, dtype, nodata, crs, res_x, res_y, xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax, n_levels, tile_width, tile_height. per-overview detail (level_width etc.) collapsed n_levels; inspect single source full vector.

title: Tidy one-row-per-source summary of cog_info metadata — as.data.frame.cog_info
code: # S3 method for class 'cog_info' as.data.frame(x, row.names = NULL, optional = FALSE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'cog_info_list' as.data.frame(x, row.names = NULL, optional = FALSE, ...)

title: Tidy one-row-per-source summary of cog_info metadata — as.data.frame.cog_info
text: x cog_info cog_info_list cog_info(). row.names, optional Unused; present .data.frame() generic compatibility. ... Unused.

title: Tidy one-row-per-source summary of cog_info metadata — as.data.frame.cog_info
text: data frame, one row per source.

what: Fetch many grouped sources through one pool, with structure-preserving output — ck_batch
title: Fetch many grouped sources through one pool, with structure-preserving output — ck_batch
text: ck_batch() takes list groups source URLs – e.g. one group per acquisition, group band-level asset URLs acquisition – fetches area--interest window every asset across every group single global concurrency pool. One pool whole job saturates network far better reading group separately, returned paths mirror input structure onward tasks know output .

title: Fetch many grouped sources through one pool, with structure-preserving output — ck_batch
code: ck_batch(   src,   dst = tempfile(fileext = ".tif"),   stack = FALSE,   t_srs = NULL,   te = NULL,   te_srs = NULL,   tr = NULL,   ts = NULL,   tap = TRUE,   r = c("near", "bilinear", "cubic", "cubicspline", "lanczos", "average", "rms", "mode",     "max", "min", "med", "q1", "q3", "sum"),   bands = NULL,   cl_arg = character(0),   num_threads = 1L,   warp_memory = "auto",   cache_max = "auto",   co = c("COMPRESS=DEFLATE", "TILED=YES", "NUM_THREADS=ALL_CPUS", "BIGTIFF=IF_SAFER"),   config = NULL,   skip_nosource = TRUE,   overview = NULL,   margin = 8L,   io_concurrency = 16L,   prefetch = NULL,   max_bytes = NULL,   sanitise = TRUE )

title: Fetch many grouped sources through one pool, with structure-preserving output — ck_batch
text: src list; element character vector source URLs/paths belong together (typically one acquisition's band assets). List names (inner vector names) used name outputs; see dst. dst Output location. Either explicit path structure matching output (list character vectors stack = FALSE, character vector length length(src) stack = TRUE), single string used path template: unique output paths derived using group names (band names) src, falling back positional indices names absent. May /vsi* path. Defaults temp-file template. stack Logical. FALSE (default) writes one file per band; TRUE stacks group's bands one file. t_srs Target SRS (e.g. "EPSG:3857"). NULL, source CRS used (reprojection). te Target extent c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax), te_srs given, otherwise t_srs. supplied AOI drives fetch. te_srs SRS te (defaults t_srs). tr Target resolution c(xres, yres) target CRS units. Takes precedence ts: given, ts ignored (warning). ts Target size c(width, height) pixels. Ignored tr also supplied. tap TRUE (default) tr given, align output pixel boundaries tr grid (gdalwarp -tap), anchored CRS origin, outputs resolution share grid stack cleanly. snaps output extent outward, longer exactly te. effect ts copy path. explicit -tap cl_arg always honoured. (warp_remote() never adds ; pass -tap .) r Resampling method (used warp path). Either single method whole batch, flat vector length equal total number assets, list mirroring src per-band method (e.g. "near" mask bands, "bilinear" reflectance). bands 1-based source bands read (default: ). Subsetting happens fetch, bands' bytes streamed. cl_arg Character vector extra raw gdalwarp flags, forwarded verbatim gdalraster::warp() (e.g. c("-et", "0")). merged flags cptkirk builds named arguments . num_threads GDAL warp threads per asset. Defaults 1 (unlike ck_warp()): assets warped one time small windows, per-warp thread spin-costs saves – "ALL_CPUS" measured ~8x slower many-small-files pattern. warp_memory Warp working memory MB (GDAL -wm). "auto" (default) uses ~25% system RAM, clamped 256-2048 MB (bigger means fewer, larger chunks); NULL defers GDAL's default; number sets explicitly. cache_max GDAL block cache MB call. "auto" (default) uses ~25% RAM, clamped 256-2000 MB; NULL defers ambient GDAL_CACHEMAX (env / gdalraster::set_config_option()); number sets explicitly. value restored previous setting afterwards. co GDAL output creation options, defaulting cloud-friendly GeoTIFF set: c("COMPRESS=DEFLATE", "TILED=YES", "NUM_THREADS=ALL_CPUS", "BIGTIFF=IF_SAFER") — losslessly compressed, tiled (output can re-read cptkirk COG reader), compressed parallel, promoted BigTIFF might exceed 4 GB. Pass override (e.g. add "PREDICTOR=2" integer data, "COMPRESS=ZSTD" GDAL built ), NULL creation options. target GTiff/COG drivers; set co output formats. config Named character vector / list extra GDAL config options set duration call (restored exit). skip_nosource TRUE (default), pass SKIP_NOSOURCE=YES + INIT_DEST warper skips output chunks source coverage (e.g. nodata margins reprojection). effect output fully covered. Ignored copy fast-path. overview Force 1-based IFD/overview level instead auto-selecting output resolution. 1 = full resolution. margin Source-pixel margin added around computed window cover resampling kernel reprojection slop (default 8). io_concurrency Number concurrent tile reads – width single global fetch pool shared across source tiles. Default 16, suits object stores throttle around many simultaneous range requests (e.g. S3 / source.coop). Raise (24-32) fast, stable link; lower store rate-limits. prefetch Streaming buffer depth: many completed windows may queue ahead warp fetch pool throttles. NULL (default) uses io_concurrency. larger value lets fetch run ahead absorb consumer (warp) jitter sustain network saturation, cost buffered windows held memory (≈ prefetch × window size). max_bytes Safety ceiling (bytes) staged -memory window (width * height * bands * <native dtype bytes>). NULL (default) uses ~1/3 system RAM. guards foot-gun warping whole large multi-band raster native resolution; narrow request, coarsen tr/ts, raise allow . sanitise Logical. modes open every source one shared connection pool per host (batch pays ~one TLS handshake, one per source). TRUE (default) validates request plans/verifies every source's grid (handles mixed grids). FALSE trusted fast path: plans window single source assumes sources share grid, skipping per-source planning probe. Use inputs known share one grid (e.g. assets one MGRS tile); -grid sources may fail , extent happens contain window, return wrong pixels.

title: Fetch many grouped sources through one pool, with structure-preserving output — ck_batch
text: output paths, mirroring input structure: list character vectors stack = FALSE, named character vector stack = TRUE. Outputs NA source overlap AOI, fetch failed (latter emits warning; stack = TRUE single failed band leaves whole group NA).

title: Fetch many grouped sources through one pool, with structure-preserving output — ck_batch
text: Output granularity set stack: stack = FALSE (default): one file per band. return value mirrors full nested input – list (one element per group) character vectors (one path per band). tools bundle bands (e.g. vrtility's per-timestamp VRTs) expect. stack = TRUE: one band-separated file per group (see ck_stack()). return value character vector, one path per group. single AOI (te / t_srs / tr / ts) applies whole batch; source clips grid, groups different tiles fine.

title: Fetch many grouped sources through one pool, with structure-preserving output — ck_batch
text: fetch always uses cptkirk's single connection pool. per-output warp + write dispatched across ambient mirai daemon pool one running (mirai mori installed mirai::daemons(n) active), runs inline otherwise. helps warp/write backs behind fetch (slow links, large many-band warps). argument: start daemons enable, mirai::daemons(0) disable. Fetched window buffers handed daemons zero-copy via mori, daemon warps single-threaded. cptkirk never spawns tears daemons.

what: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG straight into an R array — ck_read
title: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG straight into an R array — ck_read
text: ck_read() ck_warp() hands back pixels instead writing file: streams tiles request touches, warps GDAL (engine, sanitiser defaults ck_warp()), returns result base-R matrix array. Use quick extraction inspection without round-tripping file disk.

title: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG straight into an R array — ck_read
code: ck_read(   src,   t_srs = NULL,   te = NULL,   te_srs = NULL,   tr = NULL,   ts = NULL,   tap = TRUE,   r = c("near", "bilinear", "cubic", "cubicspline", "lanczos", "average", "rms", "mode",     "max", "min", "med", "q1", "q3", "sum"),   bands = NULL,   cl_arg = character(0),   num_threads = "ALL_CPUS",   warp_memory = "auto",   cache_max = "auto",   config = NULL,   skip_nosource = TRUE,   overview = NULL,   margin = 8L,   io_concurrency = 16L,   max_bytes = NULL,   sanitise = TRUE )

title: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG straight into an R array — ck_read
text: src Path/URL source GeoTIFF / COG, cog_source() handle, character vector several sources mosaic (tile's overlapping window fetched set reprojected/mosaicked one warp; non-overlapping tiles skipped). Passing cog_source() reuses already-open handle, skipping metadata re-read – worthwhile warping many AOIs source. t_srs Target SRS (e.g. "EPSG:3857"). NULL, source CRS used (reprojection). te Target extent c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax), te_srs given, otherwise t_srs. supplied AOI drives fetch. te_srs SRS te (defaults t_srs). tr Target resolution c(xres, yres) target CRS units. Takes precedence ts: given, ts ignored (warning). ts Target size c(width, height) pixels. Ignored tr also supplied. tap TRUE (default) tr given, align output pixel boundaries tr grid (gdalwarp -tap), anchored CRS origin, outputs resolution share grid stack cleanly. snaps output extent outward, longer exactly te. effect ts copy path. explicit -tap cl_arg always honoured. (warp_remote() never adds ; pass -tap .) r Resampling method, one "near" (default), "bilinear", "cubic", "cubicspline", "lanczos", "average", "rms", "mode", "max", "min", "med", "q1", "q3", "sum". Matched rlang::arg_match(), typo reports valid set. (method added newer GDAL list knows can still passed via cl_arg.) bands 1-based source bands read (default: ). Subsetting happens fetch, bands' bytes streamed. cl_arg Character vector extra raw gdalwarp flags, forwarded verbatim gdalraster::warp() (e.g. c("-et", "0")). merged flags cptkirk builds named arguments . num_threads Value GDAL's warp NUM_THREADS warp option GDAL_NUM_THREADS config (default "ALL_CPUS"), parallelising resampling computation GeoTIFF (de)compression. NULL sets neither, deferring ambient GDAL_NUM_THREADS (env / session). warp_memory Warp working memory MB (GDAL -wm). "auto" (default) uses ~25% system RAM, clamped 256-2048 MB (bigger means fewer, larger chunks); NULL defers GDAL's default; number sets explicitly. cache_max GDAL block cache MB call. "auto" (default) uses ~25% RAM, clamped 256-2000 MB; NULL defers ambient GDAL_CACHEMAX (env / gdalraster::set_config_option()); number sets explicitly. value restored previous setting afterwards. config Named character vector / list extra GDAL config options set duration call (restored exit). skip_nosource TRUE (default), pass SKIP_NOSOURCE=YES + INIT_DEST warper skips output chunks source coverage (e.g. nodata margins reprojection). effect output fully covered. Ignored copy fast-path. overview Force 1-based IFD/overview level instead auto-selecting output resolution. 1 = full resolution. margin Source-pixel margin added around computed window cover resampling kernel reprojection slop (default 8). io_concurrency Number concurrent tile reads – width single global fetch pool shared across source tiles. Default 16, suits object stores throttle around many simultaneous range requests (e.g. S3 / source.coop). Raise (24-32) fast, stable link; lower store rate-limits. max_bytes Safety ceiling (bytes) returned array, sized nrow * ncol * nbands * 8 (R stores numerics f64). NULL (default) uses ~1/3 system RAM. Coarsen tr/ts, narrow te, select fewer bands, raise read larger window. sanitise TRUE (default), validate warp arguments tiny metadata-derived stand-fetching, bad CRS, resampling method, creation option unknown flag fails milliseconds instead remote read. Set FALSE skip check.

title: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG straight into an R array — ck_read
text: numeric matrix (single band) array dimensions [nrow, ncol, nband] (multi-band), carrying geotransform, crs (set) nodata attributes self-describing convertible raster classes. R type follows output data type (integer double).

title: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG straight into an R array — ck_read
text: Two internal paths, chosen automatically: Warp (usual case – reprojection / resolution change requested): result materialised uncompressed /vsimem GeoTIFF read back gdalraster. Native window (t_srs/tr/ts, single source): fetched native bytes decoded straight R array, GDAL round-trip .

what: Stack many sources into one band-separated raster (buildvrt -separate) — ck_stack
title: Stack many sources into one band-separated raster (buildvrt -separate) — ck_stack
text: ck_stack() fetches window several sources cptkirk's single global concurrency pool (every source opened concurrently, every tile fetched one io budget) writes stacked separate bands: source contributes consecutive band(s) output, input order. streaming-remote analogue gdalbuildvrt -separate followed materialising read.

title: Stack many sources into one band-separated raster (buildvrt -separate) — ck_stack
code: ck_stack(   src,   dst,   t_srs = NULL,   te = NULL,   te_srs = NULL,   tr = NULL,   ts = NULL,   tap = TRUE,   r = c("near", "bilinear", "cubic", "cubicspline", "lanczos", "average", "rms", "mode",     "max", "min", "med", "q1", "q3", "sum"),   bands = NULL,   band_names = NULL,   cl_arg = character(0),   num_threads = "ALL_CPUS",   warp_memory = "auto",   cache_max = "auto",   co = c("COMPRESS=DEFLATE", "TILED=YES", "NUM_THREADS=ALL_CPUS", "BIGTIFF=IF_SAFER"),   config = NULL,   skip_nosource = TRUE,   overview = NULL,   margin = 8L,   io_concurrency = 16L,   max_bytes = NULL,   sanitise = TRUE )

title: Stack many sources into one band-separated raster (buildvrt -separate) — ck_stack
text: src Path/URL source GeoTIFF / COG, cog_source() handle, character vector several sources mosaic (tile's overlapping window fetched set reprojected/mosaicked one warp; non-overlapping tiles skipped). Passing cog_source() reuses already-open handle, skipping metadata re-read – worthwhile warping many AOIs source. dst Output filename (regular path, /vsimem/ path, anything GDAL can write). format inferred GDAL set -cl_arg. t_srs Target SRS (e.g. "EPSG:3857"). NULL, source CRS used (reprojection). te Target extent c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax), te_srs given, otherwise t_srs. supplied AOI drives fetch. te_srs SRS te (defaults t_srs). tr Target resolution c(xres, yres) target CRS units. Takes precedence ts: given, ts ignored (warning). ts Target size c(width, height) pixels. Ignored tr also supplied. tap TRUE (default) tr given, align output pixel boundaries tr grid (gdalwarp -tap), anchored CRS origin, outputs resolution share grid stack cleanly. snaps output extent outward, longer exactly te. effect ts copy path. explicit -tap cl_arg always honoured. (warp_remote() never adds ; pass -tap .) r Resampling method (used warp path; native-window copy ignores ). Either single method applied every source, vector length length(src) giving per-source method (e.g. "near" mask band, "bilinear" reflectance). bands 1-based source bands read (default: ). Subsetting happens fetch, bands' bytes streamed. band_names Optional character vector naming output bands, length equal total number output bands (sum selected bands across sources). NULL (default) derives name per band source file name (_b<k> suffix added multi-band sources). Names written GDAL band descriptions; carry identity , data. cl_arg Character vector extra raw gdalwarp flags, forwarded verbatim gdalraster::warp() (e.g. c("-et", "0")). merged flags cptkirk builds named arguments . num_threads Value GDAL's warp NUM_THREADS warp option GDAL_NUM_THREADS config (default "ALL_CPUS"), parallelising resampling computation GeoTIFF (de)compression. NULL sets neither, deferring ambient GDAL_NUM_THREADS (env / session). warp_memory Warp working memory MB (GDAL -wm). "auto" (default) uses ~25% system RAM, clamped 256-2048 MB (bigger means fewer, larger chunks); NULL defers GDAL's default; number sets explicitly. cache_max GDAL block cache MB call. "auto" (default) uses ~25% RAM, clamped 256-2000 MB; NULL defers ambient GDAL_CACHEMAX (env / gdalraster::set_config_option()); number sets explicitly. value restored previous setting afterwards. co GDAL output creation options, defaulting cloud-friendly GeoTIFF set: c("COMPRESS=DEFLATE", "TILED=YES", "NUM_THREADS=ALL_CPUS", "BIGTIFF=IF_SAFER") — losslessly compressed, tiled (output can re-read cptkirk COG reader), compressed parallel, promoted BigTIFF might exceed 4 GB. Pass override (e.g. add "PREDICTOR=2" integer data, "COMPRESS=ZSTD" GDAL built ), NULL creation options. target GTiff/COG drivers; set co output formats. config Named character vector / list extra GDAL config options set duration call (restored exit). skip_nosource TRUE (default), pass SKIP_NOSOURCE=YES + INIT_DEST warper skips output chunks source coverage (e.g. nodata margins reprojection). effect output fully covered. Ignored copy fast-path. overview Force 1-based IFD/overview level instead auto-selecting output resolution. 1 = full resolution. margin Source-pixel margin added around computed window cover resampling kernel reprojection slop (default 8). io_concurrency Number concurrent tile reads – width single global fetch pool shared across source tiles. Default 16, suits object stores throttle around many simultaneous range requests (e.g. S3 / source.coop). Raise (24-32) fast, stable link; lower store rate-limits. max_bytes Safety ceiling (bytes) staged -memory window (width * height * bands * <native dtype bytes>). NULL (default) uses ~1/3 system RAM. guards foot-gun warping whole large multi-band raster native resolution; narrow request, coarsen tr/ts, raise allow . sanitise TRUE (default), validate warp arguments tiny metadata-derived stand-fetching, bad CRS, resampling method, creation option unknown flag fails milliseconds instead remote read. Set FALSE skip check.

title: Stack many sources into one band-separated raster (buildvrt -separate) — ck_stack
text: dst, invisibly. dst may file path /vsi* path GDAL can write (e.g. /vsimem/stack.tif).

title: Stack many sources into one band-separated raster (buildvrt -separate) — ck_stack
text: differs passing several sources ck_warp(), mosaics (overlapping pixels blend, last source wins). Use ck_stack() bring, e.g., per-band single-band assets one acquisition single multi-band raster. Two internal paths, chosen automatically: Stack (usual case – sources already share grid reprojection/resolution change requested): fetched native windows stacked directly -separate written . Sources assumed aligned; supply t_srs/te/tr force common grid otherwise. Warp--stack: reprojection resolution change requested, source warped common target grid first, stacked, bands register exactly.

text: ck_stack_read() ck_stack() hands back pixels instead writing file: stacks sources separate bands (see ck_stack()) uncompressed /vsimem raster reads back base-R array.

what: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG with cptkirk's defaults (recommended) — ck_warp
title: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG with cptkirk's defaults (recommended) — ck_warp
text: recommended, batteries-included entry point. ck_warp() takes grid-defining gdalwarp options named arguments (t_srs, te, tr, ts, r, bands), layers cptkirk's performance defaults (multi-threaded warp, generous warp memory block cache, SKIP_NOSOURCE), streams pixels request touches async-tiff handing warp GDAL via gdalraster::warp(). faithful, defaults-free sibling gdalraster::warp(), see warp_remote().

title: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG with cptkirk's defaults (recommended) — ck_warp
text: Every actual resampling / reprojection decision GDAL's; cptkirk sizes saturates fetch.

title: Inspect a (remote) GeoTIFF / COG — cog_info
text: Reads GeoTIFF header IFDs remote-capable reader (local path, http(s)://, s3://, gs://, az://) returns georeferencing structure. pixel data fetched.

title: Inspect a (remote) GeoTIFF / COG — cog_info
Inspect a (remote) GeoTIFF / COG — cog_info
text: src Path/URL GeoTIFF / Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFF, cog_source() handle, character vector several sources. .data.frame TRUE, return one-row-per-source data frame (see .data.frame.cog_info()) instead cog_info object(s). Default FALSE.

title: Inspect a (remote) GeoTIFF / COG — cog_info
text: single source, list class cog_info width, height, n_bands, dtype (GDAL type name), nodata, geotransform (GDAL corner-based affine, length 6), crs (string GDAL can import), band_names, per-level pixel sizes level_width / level_height (level 1 = full resolution, rest overviews) plus tile_width / tile_height. several sources, cog_info_list objects. .data.frame = TRUE, data frame one row per source.

title: Inspect a (remote) GeoTIFF / COG — cog_info
text: Pass several sources inspect set – metadata reads run concurrently, result cog_info_list whose print method summarises set (shared vs varying CRS / resolution / bands, combined extent) – handy vetting tiles mosaic. Convert either form tidy one-row-per-source data frame .data.frame() (.data.frame = TRUE).

what: Open a (remote) GeoTIFF / COG once and reuse it — cog_source
title: Open a (remote) GeoTIFF / COG once and reuse it — cog_source
text: Opens source remote-capable reader (local path, http(s)://, s3://, gs://, az://), reading header IFDs single time. returned handle can passed cog_info() warp_remote() place URL, repeated warps raster (e.g. many AOIs, set rasters) pay metadata round-trips .

title: Open a (remote) GeoTIFF / COG once and reuse it — cog_source
Open a (remote) GeoTIFF / COG once and reuse it — cog_source
text: src Path URL GeoTIFF / Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFF.

title: Open a (remote) GeoTIFF / COG once and reuse it — cog_source
text: object class cog_source wrapping open handle.

title: Open a (remote) GeoTIFF / COG once and reuse it — cog_source
text: Remote object-store sources (s3://, gs://, az://) authenticate process environment, using variable names object_store documents (e.g. AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AWS_SESSION_TOKEN, AWS_REGION; GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT; AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME). static credentials builders fall back platform chain (web-identity, ECS, EC2 instance metadata). public buckets set AWS_SKIP_SIGNATURE=true (just use https:// URL). Credentials never passed R, stay scripts logs. independent GDAL's /vsi* credential settings, cptkirk use reading.

what: cptkirk: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs — cptkirk-package
title: cptkirk: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs — cptkirk-package
text: remote, overview-aware gdalwarp. cptkirk works source pixels requested warp touch, streams tiles Rust async-tiff reader (high remote-read saturation, appropriate overview level), stages -memory GDAL source, hands actual reprojection resampling GDAL via gdalraster — following gdalwarp argument interface.

title: cptkirk: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs — cptkirk-package
text: Two entry points onto engine: ck_warp() — recommended, batteries-included helper: named t_srs / te / tr / ts / r / bands arguments plus cptkirk's performance defaults (multi-threading, generous warp memory cache, SKIP_NOSOURCE, band-subset streaming). warp_remote() — faithful, defaults-free sibling gdalraster::warp(): src / dst / t_srs / cl_arg call shape, source streamed remotely. Forwards cl_arg GDAL verbatim. ck_read() — ck_warp() returns result R matrix/array instead writing file, quick extraction inspection.

title: cptkirk: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs — cptkirk-package
text: engine drives many sources one saturating pool: ck_stack() / ck_stack_read() — fetch one window N sources write (return) separate bands single output: gdalbuildvrt -separate analogue, opposed mosaic ck_warp() makes multiple sources. ck_batch() — reproject/resample whole list groups (e.g. STAC item list, one group per acquisition) ONE connection pool ONE streaming fetch, writing one file per band one stacked file per group. output mirrors structure input.

title: cptkirk: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs — cptkirk-package
text: cog_info() — read source's structure georeferencing (header IFDs ; pixels fetched) cog_source() — open source reuse handle across calls

title: cptkirk: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs — cptkirk-package
text: gdalwarp-style request cptkirk: reprojects target extent source's CRS find pixel window source must supply; selects overview level matching output resolution; streams just tiles concurrently async-tiff (one global concurrency pool across tiles); stages /vsimem lets GDAL perform warp, mosaicking multiple source tiles single pass. Across multiple sources opens share one connection pool per host — batch pays roughly one TLS handshake per host, one per source — window warped soon lands, overlapping fetches still flight. reimplements none GDAL's warp logic; sizes saturates fetch. Sources may local paths http(s)://, s3://, gs://, az:// URLs.

title: cptkirk: Warp-Speed Remote GDAL Warping of Cloud-Optimised GeoTIFFs — cptkirk-package
text: Maintainer: Hugh Graham hugh@belian.earth Authors: Hugh Graham hugh@belian.earth

what: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG (a streaming sibling of gdalraster::warp) — warp_remote
title: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG (a streaming sibling of gdalraster::warp) — warp_remote
text: near drop-gdalraster::warp() reads source(s) remotely. call shape – src, dst, t_srs, raw gdalwarp argument vector cl_arg – instead letting GDAL pull source /vsicurl, cptkirk works pixels request touches streams just tiles Rust async-tiff reader (appropriate overview level), stages /vsimem, hands warp GDAL. Output identical gdalraster::warp() source.

title: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG (a streaming sibling of gdalraster::warp) — warp_remote
code: warp_remote(   src,   dst,   t_srs = "",   cl_arg = NULL,   quiet = TRUE,   overview = NULL,   margin = 8L,   io_concurrency = 16L,   max_bytes = NULL,   sanitise = TRUE )

title: Warp a remote GeoTIFF / COG (a streaming sibling of gdalraster::warp) — warp_remote
text: src Path/URL source GeoTIFF / COG, cog_source() handle, character vector several sources mosaic. cog_source() reuses already-open handle, skipping metadata re-read. dst Output filename (regular path, /vsimem/, anything GDAL can write). format inferred GDAL set -cl_arg. t_srs Target SRS (e.g. "EPSG:3857"), gdalraster::warp(). "" (default) NULL means reprojection (source CRS). cl_arg Character vector raw gdalwarp flags, forwarded verbatim gdalraster::warp() (e.g. c("-te", "0", "0", "100", "100", "-tr", "10", "10", "-r", "bilinear")). cptkirk parses -te/-te_srs/-tr/-ts purely size fetch. quiet TRUE (default), GDAL warp runs without progress callback. Unlike gdalraster::warp() defaults TRUE: progress callback invoked GDAL's worker threads (e.g. -multi) can crash R session. overview Force 1-based IFD/overview level instead auto-selecting output resolution. 1 = full resolution. margin Source-pixel margin added around computed window cover resampling kernel reprojection slop (default 8). io_concurrency Number concurrent tile reads – width single global fetch pool shared across source tiles (default 16). max_bytes Safety ceiling (bytes) staged -memory window. NULL (default) uses ~1/3 system RAM. sanitise TRUE (default), validate cl_arg (t_srs) tiny metadata-derived stand-fetching, bad CRS, resampling method, creation option unknown flag fails milliseconds instead remote read. Set FALSE skip check.
